behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: validator + intake orchestrator must stop downstream calls and aggregate all missing fields. Unit tests of validator logic alone miss the 'no downstream processing' guarantee. Higher levels are disqualified because no real OS/network/LLM behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with missing fields using in-process fakes for insurance/triage/scheduling; assert returned error lists every missing field and assert downstream services were not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure local validation/parsing rule in one function/object boundary; no module interaction is needed to expose failure. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text (only DOB rule checks).
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests over DOB inputs (future, >150 years old, invalid string, valid boundary cases) asserting accept/reject outcomes and error messages.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior crosses real HTTP API boundary; request encoding/transport details and status mapping can fail in ways in-process mocks hide. Integration was considered but rejected because concrete behavior text requires sending data to external payer API.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real HTTP test server (or sandbox payer endpoint) to capture outbound request and return each eligibility response; assert request contains correct patient ID/plan code and responses map to internal states correctly.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real network failure modes (timeout/connection refusal/DNS) at API boundary. Mocked exceptions can miss retry/timeout stack behavior. Higher Agentic/Workflow levels are disqualified: no model-output correctness and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real connectivity failure to payer endpoint (closed port/timeout) in end-to-end intake path; assert eligibility becomes `pending` and workflow proceeds to triage/scheduling.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: This tests payload construction/orchestration around the LLM call, not model judgment. Per AI-CALL-DEFAULT exception, infra-around-call behaviors can be Integration with stubbed endpoint. System rejected: real network not required to catch missing fields in payload.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with stubbed model client; assert outbound payload includes exact reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric fields.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality/decision behavior; deterministic stubs always pass and hide true failure. Workflow rejected because behavior requires one model decision, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom cases and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold; track variance across runs.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser-to-storage handoff: explanation may parse but not persist (or schema mapping may drop it). Unit parser test alone can miss persistence integration issues. Agentic rejected because behavior is about field handling, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage responses into parsing + persistence path (stubbed model output), then read stored record and assert explanation text is present and non-empty alongside score.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is model-output quality (specificity/rationale), which mocks cannot validate. Workflow rejected: only a single agentic generation is under test, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on varied symptom cases; score explanations for explicit mention of case symptoms and urgency reasoning, and fail generic/template-like responses.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduler logic + calendar DB/module interaction to verify slot selection policy and availability lookup. No real OS/network/LLM dependency in the behavior text, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with controlled availability, run scheduler for high/medium urgency, and assert chosen slot is first valid one meeting same-day/48h constraints.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Race condition emerges when scheduler and shared DB interact under concurrent requests; unit logic is insufficient. System was considered, but behavior can be reproduced with local DB + concurrent workers in-process, so real external infra is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling transactions against the same local calendar DB slot; assert one succeeds and the other is rejected/reassigned, with no duplicate booking persisted.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Requires data assembly across scheduling/provider/visit-type modules; template-only unit tests can miss missing-field wiring defects. Higher levels disqualified: no real network/OS and no model-output dependency stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute confirmation composition from persisted appointment context and visit type rules; assert rendered message contains required fields and correct prep instructions per visit type.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly an orchestration test with deterministic stubs; failure boundary is module sequencing/state handoff. Agentic rejected by behavior text ('deterministic stubbed triage output'). System rejected unless real network/subprocess is required, which this behavior does not require.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline in-process with stubbed insurance/triage responses and local DB; assert each stage executes in order and final confirmation is produced with expected state transitions.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Execution path includes live AI triage; per AI-CALL-DEFAULT this is Agentic unless testing only transport, which this is not. Workflow considered but rejected because behavior specifies one agentic step within pipeline, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real intake through production-like environment using live payer API and live triage model; assert unattended completion through confirmation with valid persisted outputs.
